text: . We present a new benchmark for object classification by placing the object classification problem in the perspective of the more general problem of scene understanding. Our approach is to gather a new image dataset of cluttered real-world scenes (photographs) that contain various objects (things) commonly seen in everyday environments. The objects are localized using per-instance segmentation. We have chosen objects that a 4 year old would be able to identify easily. The OpenImagesDataset has 91 classes of objects with 2.5M labeled instances across 328K images that were collected with the aid of thousands of human labelers who were assigned various tasks that were facilitated using innovative user interfaces for the object classification, object localization and object segmentation tasks. We give some insights in the distribution of the data using several statistics including frequency distributions and scatterplots which provide valuable information about our dataset when compared to other common datasets such as PASCAL, ImageNet and SUN. We evaluate baseline performance for the object classification task using the deformable parts model along with various evaluations on the localization and segmentation aspects of the detection task. 1
